subject: PATIENT
heading: Hospital Course:__page_no__8
text: hospital course: 89 yo very pleasant male. he has a history of chronic stable heart disease. he has recently had some cognitive decline. he had hammertoe surgery and was taking tramadol for pains. he had an episode of ams witnessed by wife with lip smacking. he came to er, he was admitted for observation. his neuro exam is normal, no focal signs. speech fluent, some cognitive impairment at baseline. imaging no cva. cardiac status stable. he was briefly hypoxic in er, weaned off o2. he is re-examined with wife, patient at baseline cognition. it seems the ams was due to tramadol. it is suspected he has an element of vascular dementia. patient was examined with clinical rn and patients wife, followup pcp and cardiologist.

text: hospital course: 89 yo very pleasant male. he has a history of chronic stable heart disease. he has recently had some cognitive decline. he had hammertoe surgery and was taking tramadol for pains. he had an episode of ams witnessed by wife with lip smacking. he came to er, he was admitted for observation. his neuro exam is normal, no focal signs. speech fluent, some cognitive impairment at baseline. imaging no cva. cardiac status stable. he was briefly hypoxic in er, weaned off o2. he is re-examined with wife, patient at baseline cognition. it seems the ams was due to tramadol. it is suspected he has an element of vascular dementia. patient was examined with clinical rn and patients wife, followup pcp and cardiologist.
